codeReview.summary: Typed planner-input and deterministic precedence logic exist, but the VAL-DAEMON-008 behavior required by feature/contract is not implemented, and the worker handoff commit points to unrelated m2 validation files rather than m3 planner-input changes.

issues.description: VAL-DAEMON-008 requires unsupported workflow intent to fail pre-execution with validation error/no run start, but unsupported workflow types are silently skipped (`return nil`) and then normalized via lower-precedence fallback, allowing planning/execution to continue.

Tests for VAL-DAEMON-008 assert fallback/no-throw behavior (`testPlannerUnsupportedWorkflowIntentFallsBackGracefully` and `...DoesNotThrow`) instead of asserting fail-pre-execution behavior, so the suite currently codifies behavior opposite to the validation contract.

Handoff commitId (`62ef19b8`) does not contain m3 typed-planner-input code changes; `git show --name-only 62ef19b8` touches only `.factory/validation/m2-authoring-ui-surface/...` artifacts. This breaks feature traceability for this worker session.

sharedStateObservations.observation: mission-core-worker SKILL validator command snippets use `OpenBurnBar*` test filters that do not match current BurnBar XCTest class names, which led this worker to run zero-match filters before switching.
Worker handoff accepted a commitId unrelated to the assigned feature implementation, indicating commit-trace verification is not reliably enforced by current worker procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff file line 6 sets commitId=62ef19b8; `git show --name-only 62ef19b8` contains only m2 validation artifacts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Validation assertion/source-of-truth drift exists for VAL-DAEMON-008: contract text requires pre-execution failure, while implementation/tests enforce graceful fallback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: validation-contract.md:44-47 specifies 'returns validation error and no run starts'; OpenBurnBarPlannerService.swift:133-135 returns nil for unsupported workflow; OpenBurnBarAgentStackTests.swift:653-707 asserts fallback and no throw.

summary: Review failed: VAL-DAEMON-008 behavior is inverted relative to feature/contract requirements, and the reviewed worker handoff commit is unrelated to m3 typed planner-input changes, so implementation traceability and contract compliance are not met.
